heading: Preemption of State and local laws
label: 4122 Preemption of State and local laws
notes: Amendments1992—Subsec. (b). substituted “, such as any law or regulation” for “and” after “subchapter”.Pub. L. 102–550
amendments: Amendments1992—Subsec. (b). substituted “, such as any law or regulation” for “and” after “subchapter”.Pub. L. 102–550

children.text: restricts or inhibits the prepayment of any mortgage described in (or the voluntary termination of any insurance contract pursuant to ) on eligible low income housing;section 4119(1) of this titlesection 1715t of this title
restricts or inhibits an owner of such housing from receiving the authorized annual return provided under ;section 4104 of this title

is inconsistent with any provision of this subchapter, including any law, regulation, or other restriction that limits or impairs the ability of any owner of eligible low income housing to receive incentives authorized under this subchapter (including authorization to increase rental rates, transfer the housing, obtain secondary financing, or use the proceeds of any of such incentives); or
in its applicability to low-income housing is limited only to eligible low-income housing for which the owner has prepaid the mortgage or terminated the insurance contract.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: b Effect
children.text: This section shall not prevent the establishment, continuing in effect, or enforcement of any law or regulation of any State or political subdivision of a State not inconsistent with the provisions of this subchapter, such as any law or regulation relating to building standards, zoning limitations, health, safety, or habitability standards for housing, rent control, or conversion of rental housing to condominium or cooperative ownership, to the extent such law or regulation is of general applicability to both housing receiving Federal assistance and nonassisted housing. This section shall not preempt, annul, or alter any contractual restrictions or obligations existing before , that prevent or limit an owner of eligible low-income housing from prepaying the mortgage on the housing (or terminating the insurance contract on the housing).November 28, 1990
